selector_output: transmitted keyframe indices or segment lengths

feature_source_audit.example_features: frame RGB, grayscale, downsampled RGB, frame timestamp/index
feature_source_audit.source: input video frames available to encoder; DAVIS RGB files in current experiments
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: feed-forward for offline video encoding; for online streaming requires declared lookahead window
feature_source_audit.metadata_transmitted: only selected keyframe indices/schedule, not RGB features
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: dataset RGB is the source signal being compressed, not extra side information

feature_source_audit.example_features: frame difference, block SAD/MSE, edge-change, histogram difference, local gradient magnitude
feature_source_audit.source: deterministic functions of encoder input RGB frames
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: feed-forward if computed only from input frames inside declared lookahead
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: preferred first heuristic selector feature family

feature_source_audit.example_features: RAFT/GMFlow optical flow, DINO/ResNet frame embeddings, learned motion confidence
feature_source_audit.source: fixed pretrained network fed only encoder input RGB frames
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: feed-forward with compute-cost caveat; no target dense anchors or rendered metrics may enter
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: network compute and dependency must be documented; no bitrate charge unless features are transmitted

feature_source_audit.example_features: candidate Stage158 residual payload bytes, candidate original StreamSplat middle residual estimates
feature_source_audit.source: encoder-side candidate encodes/renders using available input and target anchors during compression
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: offline encoder-side RD probe, not a cheap single-pass feed-forward selector
feature_source_audit.metadata_transmitted: keyframe schedule plus normal Stage158 payloads
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: must not be described as low-compute feed-forward; useful upper/teacher policy

feature_source_audit.example_features: rendered PSNR, SSIM, MS-SSIM, LPIPS, bad-case rank
feature_source_audit.source: requires target RGB and rendered candidate output
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: not feed-forward selector input for deployable inference
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: allowed only to create labels/evaluate oracle schedules

feature_source_audit.example_features: target dense anchor, target Gaussian residual, unencoded residual tensor
feature_source_audit.source: Stage61 dense anchors or residual against target anchor
feature_source_audit.feedforward_status: not allowed as selector inference feature except when it is encoded into transmitted Stage158 residual payload
feature_source_audit.metadata_transmitted: must be actual encoded residual payload if used by decoder
feature_source_audit.cost_or_caveat: prevents oracle leakage into keyframe selector

rate_accounting.counting_rule: count q-bit Gaussian anchor payload/metadata for every transmitted keyframe
rate_accounting.notes: Use existing q12/qbit anchor accounting tables; if schedule changes keyframe count, anchor rate changes accordingly.

rate_accounting.counting_rule: count schedule metadata for non-uniform selectors: keyframe count plus sorted indices or segment lengths packed with ceil(log2(total_frames)) bits/index
rate_accounting.notes: Uniform fixed-gap schedules can be signaled by mode id only; adaptive schedules must transmit their indices or equivalent segment lengths.

rate_accounting.counting_rule: count at least one small mode id when multiple schedule policies are available
count q6/keep1.0 entropy residual payload plus one-byte half selector for every recovered middle/intermediate frame
rate_accounting.notes: Payload may vary by segment difficulty; Stage158 current policy is quality-first and not rate-minimized.

rate_accounting.counting_rule: not counted as bitrate if features are not transmitted, but compute/dependency tier must be reported
rate_accounting.notes: Pretrained optical flow or embeddings must be documented as higher-compute encoder tools.

baselines.transmitted_metadata: mode id or fixed policy id only
mode id or fixed policy id only
adaptive keyframe indices or segment lengths
baselines.status: reuse for comparison, not final without DAVIS+Stage158 validation

baselines.selector_inputs: raw RGB frame differences, block motion proxy, edge-change statistics
baselines.transmitted_metadata: adaptive keyframe indices or segment lengths

baselines.selector_inputs: RGB/motion features only; no target anchors or rendered metrics at inference
baselines.transmitted_metadata: adaptive keyframe indices or segment lengths

baselines.selector_inputs: rendered RD/quality labels from candidate schedules
baselines.transmitted_metadata: oracle schedule indices for accounting only
baselines.role: upper bound and training label source

protocol_decisions.details: Use Stage158 policy as middle/intermediate recovery component; do not over-optimize its residual rate in this phase.
Selector outputs keyframe indices or segment lengths; decoder follows transmitted schedule and does not reproduce selector features.
RGB/motion features are allowed if derived from input video frames available to encoder; source and compute tier must be logged.
Current DAVIS protocol is offline encoding, so full input RGB can be used encoder-side. Online/streaming variants must declare a lookahead window.
Target dense anchors, unencoded residuals, target RGB quality labels, rendered PSNR/LPIPS, and oracle labels are not selector inference inputs.
Report all-frame, keyframe-only, middle-only, per-gap, and per-sequence PSNR/SSIM/MS-SSIM/LPIPS plus rate.
Learned selector validation should split by sequence; heuristic selector should report all selected DAVIS sequences and weak cases.

historical_selector_artifacts.relevance: segment-error RD curve on earlier data/protocol
historical_selector_artifacts.use_in_stage162: historical comparison only; must revalidate on DAVIS + Stage158
fixed middle-recovery component for adaptive schedule evaluation

next_stage: Stage163 build DAVIS RGB/motion selector data and oracle/reference labels
